summary: The student asked how to maintain a Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP) over time. The assistant explained that DRP maintenance involves regular review and updates. Key activities include scheduling periodic reviews, conducting regular disaster recovery drills or simulations to validate the plan and identify gaps, and updating documentation whenever there are significant changes to infrastructure, personnel, business processes, or vendor information. The assistant also highlighted the importance of post-incident analysis after any actual disaster or significant IT incident to identify areas for improvement and ensure all involved personnel receive adequate training on the current DRP. The assistant used the analogy of servicing a car to illustrate the proactive nature of DRP maintenance.
